evals.prompt: There's a new issue on the NemoClaw repo, number 2147. Someone reported the CLI crashing on macOS when they try to onboard. Can you figure out what labels it should get and apply them?
evals.expected_output: Read triage-instructions.md, fetch issue 2147 via gh, suggest labels (likely bug + Platform: MacOS) with a one-sentence reason and optional triage comment, wait for approval, then apply labels and log the session.
assertions: Response indicates it will read or consult `docs/triage-instructions.md` before suggesting labels
Response fetches issue 2147 via `gh issue view` against the NVIDIA/NemoClaw repo rather than triaging from the title alone
Response suggests concrete labels appropriate to a macOS CLI crash (for example `bug` and `Platform: MacOS`) with a one-sentence reason
Response waits for user approval before applying labels rather than running `gh issue edit` immediately
Response plans to log the action to the daily-rhythm activity folder (`~/development/daily-rhythm/activity/nemoclaw-triage-log.md`) after approval
Response does NOT stage or commit the triage log file to the NemoClaw repo

evals.prompt: Can you go through all the unlabeled issues and PRs on NemoClaw and suggest labels for them? A couple of them look like they might involve credential handling or sandbox escape, so be careful with those.
evals.expected_output: Run batch mode: fetch unlabeled open issues and PRs via gh + jq filter, prioritize items with critical or security signals, present one item at a time with suggested labels and wait for approval before moving on, treating the security-adjacent items with priority and the security label per triage-instructions.
assertions: Response uses `gh issue list` and `gh pr list` with a `jq` filter that selects items where `labels | length == 0`
Response works through items one at a time, pausing for approval between each rather than bulk-applying labels
Response prioritizes items with outage, data loss, or critical breakage signals (candidates for `priority: high`) ahead of routine items
Response flags the credential-handling or sandbox-escape items as candidates for a security label and treats them with extra care per the triage instructions
Response appends a session summary (items triaged, labels applied) to the triage log at the end of the batch
Response does NOT apply labels to every fetched item in a single bulk `gh` call without per-item approval

evals.prompt: I've got some time this afternoon to work through open PRs on NemoClaw. Can you help me figure out which ones are worth reviewing first? Especially anything security-related or high priority.
evals.expected_output: Recognize this is review-candidate surfacing (find-review-pr territory) rather than labeling unlabeled items; redirect to the find-review-pr flow that lists already-labeled security and priority-high PRs linked to their issues, and do not invoke the triage labeling workflow.
assertions: Response identifies this as a review-prioritization task, not a labeling task
Response surfaces PRs that already carry `security` or `priority: high` labels rather than proposing labels for unlabeled items
Response links candidate PRs to their originating issues or notes duplicates where multiple PRs fix the same issue
Response does NOT read `docs/triage-instructions.md` or start the triage labeling workflow
Response does NOT run `gh issue list ... | jq 'select(.labels | length == 0)'` or an equivalent unlabeled-items fetch
Response does NOT call `gh pr edit --add-label` or propose applying labels to the surfaced PRs
